root.description: Whether to create SSM Parameter
root.name: create

root.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: Regular expression used to validate the parameter value
root.name: allowed_pattern

root.description: Data type of the parameter. Valid values: `text`, `aws:ssm:integration` and `aws:ec2:image` for AMI format, see the [Native parameter support for Amazon Machine Image IDs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/parameter-store-ec2-aliases.html)
root.name: data_type

root.description: Description of the parameter
root.name: description

root.description: Parameter tier to assign to the parameter. If not specified, will use the default parameter tier for the region. Valid tiers are Standard, Advanced, and Intelligent-Tiering. Downgrading an Advanced tier parameter to Standard will recreate the resource
root.name: tier

root.description: Value of the parameter. This value is always marked as sensitive in the Terraform plan output, regardless of type. Additionally, write-only values are never stored to state. `value_wo_version` can be used to trigger an update and is required with this argument
root.name: value_wo_version

root.description: Whether the type of the value should be considered as secure or not
root.name: secure_type

root.description: KMS key ID or ARN for encrypting a `SecureString`
root.name: key_id

root.description: Name of the parameter. If the name contains a path (e.g., any forward slashes (`/`)), it must be fully qualified with a leading forward slash (`/`)
root.name: name

root.description: Overwrite an existing parameter. If not specified, defaults to `false` during create operations to avoid overwriting existing resources and then `true` for all subsequent operations once the resource is managed by Terraform
root.name: overwrite

root.description: Type of the parameter. Valid types are `String`, `StringList` and `SecureString`
root.name: type

root.description: Value of the parameter
root.name: value

root.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
root.name: tags

root.description: Whether to create SSM Parameter and ignore changes in value
root.name: ignore_value_changes

root.description: List of values of the parameter (will be jsonencoded to store as string natively in SSM)
root.name: values

root.description: Insecure value of the parameter
root.name: insecure_value

root.description: Whether SSM parameter is a SecureString or not?
root.name: secure_type

root.description: Type of the parameter
root.name: ssm_parameter_type

root.description: Parameter value after jsondecode(). Probably this is what you are looking for
root.name: value

root.description: Secure value of the parameter
root.name: secure_value

root.description: The ARN of the parameter
root.name: ssm_parameter_arn

root.description: Version of the parameter
root.name: ssm_parameter_version

root.description: Name of the parameter
root.name: ssm_parameter_name

root.description: Raw value of the parameter (as it is stored in SSM). Use 'value' output to get jsondecode'd value
root.name: raw_value
